questions.question: Which Indian Standard (IS) Codes are adopted for performing the slump cone test and apparatus specifications respectively?

explanations.a: Incorrect. IS 5515 is not the correct code for apparatus specifications.
explanations.b: Incorrect. The order is reversed and IS 5515 is not the correct code.
explanations.c: Correct! IS 1199 covers the slump cone test and IS 7320 covers apparatus specifications.

questions.question: What are the dimensions of slump cone?

answers.a: Top dia=20 cm, Bottom dia=30 cm and Height=10 cm
answers.b: Top dia=10 cm, Bottom dia=30 cm and Height=20 cm
answers.c: Top dia=20 cm, Bottom dia=40 cm and Height=30 cm
answers.d: Top dia=10 cm, Bottom dia=20 cm and Height=30 cm

explanations.a: Incorrect. These are not the standard dimensions of a slump cone.
explanations.b: Incorrect. The bottom diameter and height are wrong.
explanations.c: Incorrect. The top and bottom diameters are wrong.
explanations.d: Correct! The standard slump cone has Top dia=10 cm, Bottom dia=20 cm and Height=30 cm.

questions.question: What should be the maximum size of aggregate for which the slump test can be progressed in field?

explanations.a: Incorrect. 40 mm exceeds the maximum allowed size.
explanations.b: Incorrect. 20 mm is below the maximum limit.
explanations.c: Correct! As per IS 1199, the nominal maximum size of aggregate should not exceed 38 mm.
explanations.d: Incorrect. 36 mm is not the specified maximum size.

questions.question: Workability of concrete can be related to water cement ratio as-

answers.a: Higher w/c ratio mixes offer higher workability.
answers.b: Higher w/c ratio mixes offer low workability.
answers.c: Lower w/c ratio mixes offer higher workability.

explanations.a: Correct! Higher water-cement ratio increases the workability of concrete.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Higher w/c ratio actually increases workability.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Lower w/c ratio results in stiffer mixes with lower workability.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Workability is directly related to the water-cement ratio.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a test for assessing the workability of concrete?

explanations.a: Incorrect. Compaction Factor Test is a standard workability test.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Flow Table Test is used to assess workability.
explanations.c: Correct! Le-Chatelier Test is used for testing soundness of cement, not workability of concrete.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Vee-Bee Test is a standard workability test.

questions.question: Workability of concrete can not be associated with which one of the following concepts?

answers.d: All of them are associated to concrete's workability

explanations.a: Incorrect. Ease of flow is associated with workability.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Prevention of harshness is associated with workability.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Prevention of segregation and bleeding is associated with workability.
explanations.d: Correct! All the listed concepts are indeed associated with concrete's workability.
